A 40-years old gentleman Mr. Sohan, farmer by occupation, was brought to emergency department at 11 PM in unconscious state. History revealed some spray of a pesticide in his farm by the gentleman in the evening. No other relevant medical history was there. On examination, pupils were of pin-point size. Some froths and dribbling of saliva from mouth was noticed. Secretions dribbling from eyes and nose were also found. Heart rate was 55 beats per minute and the blood pressure was 80/52 mm Hg. Based on the information provided, answer the following questions:
a) Name two pesticides used in the farms. Discuss the pharmacological basis of changing of clothes and wiping/cleaning of skin for the management of this case.
b) Draw a schematic labeled diagram to describe the mechanism of these insecticides for causing above manifestations.
c) Name the initial drug of choice to treat this patient. Also mention the dosage of this drug for management of this patient.
d) Draw a labeled diagram to explain why Pralidoxime is highly effective against few pesticides but less/not effective against others. (2+2+2+2=8)

A 52-years old lady Mrs. Dholki Devi presents to outpatient clinic with complaints of few skin lesions around the eyes. Family history revealed death of her father at the age of 49 years. On examination xanthelasmas were found surrounding both of the eyes. Investigations revealed serum cholesterol 396 mg/dL, triglyceride levels 298 mg/dL and LDL cholesterol 157 mg/dL. Based on the information provided here, answer the following questions:
a) Which one of the classes, based on mechanism of action, of hypolipidemic drugs would you like to choose to prescribe to this patient. Give four examples of drugs from this class.
b) Which one of the classes of hypolipidemic drugs would you prefer for treatment of severe hypertriglyceridemia ,serum triglycerides >1000 mg/dL. Write examples of two drugs from this class.
c) Discuss the rationality of combination of PCSK9 inhibitors with statins.
d) Enumerate four important adverse effects of statins hypolipidemic drugs. (2+1+2+2=7)

Based on the information provided, answer the following questions: a) Which one class of drugs would you like to choose for your patient to relieve as well as to prevent occurrence of episodes of chest pain. A 70-year-old male presented with complaint of dull headache, giddiness, weakness and occasional breathlessness.
